content.description:
Visual network graph of item relationships.

## Concept

- Nodes represent items
- Edges represent relationships (attachments, `item://` links, type references, tag associations)
- Interactive: click nodes to navigate, drag to rearrange
- Filterable by type, tag, or relationship kind

## Libraries to Consider

- D3.js force-directed graph
- Cytoscape.js
- Vis.js network

## Implementation Notes

Could be built as a view item — a new view type that renders a container's contents (or query results) as a force-directed graph rather than a spatial canvas or list.

## Design Questions

- Is graph view useful for personal information, or just visually appealing but not practical?
- What insights would it provide that search/tags don't?
- How to handle large graphs (hundreds of items)? Clustering? Progressive loading?

## Priority

Low — interesting visualization but unclear practical value for personal use. The [Spatial Windowing](item://c0c0c0c0-0020-0000-0000-000000000000) system already provides a spatial view of items.
